title: 2025 Session 2 exercises
body:
Session 2: Questions Question 2.1 You visit the foreign exchange trading room of a major bank. A trader asks for quotations of the British pound (USD/GBP) from various correspondents and hears the following quotes:

From Bank A: 1.6580–1.6585 From Bank B: 1.6582–1.6587

What do they mean?

Question 2.2 Here are some quotes of the Swiss franc/U.S. dollar spot exchange rate given simultaneously on the phone by three banks:

Bank A: 1.3435–1.3440 Bank B: 1.3435–1.3445 Bank C: 1.3445–1.3450

Are these quotes reasonable? Do you have an arbitrage opportunity?

Question 2.3 The euro is quoted as US$/€ = 1.1420–1.1425, and the Canadian dollar is quoted as C$/US$ = 1.3540–1.3545. What is the implicit C$/€ quotation?

Question 2.4 The euro is quoted as €/$ = 0.79610–0.79650, and the Australian dollar is quoted as A$/$ = 1.5675–1.5685. What is the implicit A$/€ quotation?

Question 2.5 Suppose Dow Chemical receives quotes of $0.009369-71 for the yen and $0.03675-6 for the Taiwan dollar (NT$).

a. How many U.S. dollars will Dow Chemical receive from the sale of ¥50 million? b. What is the U.S. dollar cost to Dow Chemical of buying ¥1 billion? c. How many NT$ will Dow Chemical receive for U.S.$500,000? d. How many yen will Dow Chemical receive for NT$200 million? e. What is the yen cost to Dow Chemical of buying NT$80 million?

Question 2.6 Suppose the euro is quoted at 0.6064- 80 in London, and the pound sterling is quoted at 1.6244-59 in Frankfurt. Is there a profitable arbitrage situation? Describe it.
